label: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 16

extraction.one_line_summary: PEDERSEN Mads won uphill sprint finish after EVENEPOEL Remco punctured inside 3km and ROGLIČ Primož crashed in sprint, retaining same time.
tactical_patterns: ROGLIČ Primož initiated decisive acceleration on 8.3% final ramps, forcing GC selection despite sprint finish context.
ACKERMANN Pascal positioned directly on ROGLIČ wheel, revealing sprinter pre-stage reconnaissance of GC attack timing on steep finish.
3km rule saved EVENEPOEL Remco from time loss when puncturing, proving late-race mechanicals less costly than mid-stage.
Peloton allowed only two-rider break all day, indicating collective certainty about sprint finish despite categorized climb.

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: neutral: punctured inside 3km but saved by rule, still fresh and leading by 1:26 despite not contesting finish
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: negative: crashed in sprint after initiating winning move, battered condition raises questions about recovery for remaining mountain stages
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: positive: won uphill sprint after shadowing ROGLIČ attack, green jersey secured, showing climbing adaptation beyond pure sprinting
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: positive: wheel-surfed ROGLIČ on steep ramps to contest finish, tactical positioning reveals transformed climbing ability
rider_signals.WRIGHT Fred: negative: caused ROGLIČ crash with rising knee contact in sprint, contested finish but tactical awareness lapsed
rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: negative: punctured before final climb, eliminating stage contention despite apparent form
